list1.aberration: the fact or an instance of deviating or being aberrant especially from a moral standard or normal state
list1.abundance: an ample quantity : an abundant amount : profusion
list1.accelerate: to move faster : to gain speed
list1.adamant: unshakable or insistent especially in maintaining a position or opinion : unyielding
list1.adversary: one that contends with, opposes, or resists : an enemy or opponent
list1.advocate: one who defends or maintains a cause or proposal
list1.aesthetic: of, relating to, or dealing with aesthetics or the beautiful
list1.ambiguous: capable of being understood in two or more possible senses or ways
list1.ambivalent: having or showing simultaneous and contradictory attitudes or feelings toward something or someone : characterized by ambivalence
list1.amenable: having or showing willingness to agree or to accept something that is wanted or asked for —usually used with to
list1.anachronistic: an error in chronology; especially : a chronological misplacing of persons, events, objects, or customs in regard to each other
list1.anomaly: something different, abnormal, peculiar, or not easily classified : something anomalous
list1.antagonist: one that contends with or opposes another : adversary, opponent
list1.apathetic: affected by, characterized by, or displaying apathy : having or showing little or no interest, concern, or emotion
list1.arbitrary: chosen, decided, etc. seemingly at random or on a whim rather than in a reasoned or methodical way; also : random
list1.archaic: having the characteristics of the language of the past and surviving chiefly in specialized uses
list1.articulate: expressing oneself readily, clearly, and effectively; also : expressed in such a manner
list1.ascetic: practicing strict self-denial as a measure of personal and especially spiritual discipline
list1.assiduous: showing great care, attention, and effort : marked by careful unremitting attention or persistent application
list1.astute: having or showing shrewdness and an ability to notice and understand things clearly : mentally sharp or clever; also : clever in the use of subtlety or strategy : crafty, wily
list1.atrophy: decrease in size or wasting away of a body part or tissue; also : arrested development or loss of a part or organ incidental to the normal development or life of an animal or plant
list1.austere: stern and cold in appearance or manner
list1.autonomous: having the right or power of self-government
list1.avarice: excessive or insatiable desire for wealth or profit : greediness, cupidity
list1.belligerent: inclined to or exhibiting assertiveness, hostility, or combativeness
list1.benevolent: marked by kindness or generosity : disposed to doing good
list1.cacophony: harsh or jarring sound : dissonance; specifically : harshness in the sound of words or phrases
list1.candid: expressing opinions, feelings, etc. in an open, honest, and sincere way; also : being or involving such expression
list1.capricious: governed or characterized by sudden irrational and unpredictable impulses or whims : impulsive; broadly : unpredictable, erratic
list1.caustic: capable of destroying or eating away by chemical action : corrosive
list1.chronological: of, relating to, or arranged in or according to the order of time; also : reckoned in units of time
list1.circumspect: careful to consider all circumstances and possible consequences : prudent
list1.cogent: appealing forcibly to the mind or reason : convincing
list1.coherent: logically or aesthetically ordered or integrated : consistent
list1.collaborate: to work jointly with others or together especially in an intellectual endeavor
list1.complacent: marked by satisfaction with the status quo especially when accompanied by unawareness of actual dangers or deficiencies : marked by complacency
list1.concise: marked by brevity of expression or statement : free from all elaboration and superfluous detail
list1.condone: to regard or treat (something bad or blameworthy) as acceptable, forgivable, or harmless
list1.conspicuous: obvious to the eye or mind
list1.corroborate: to support or help prove with evidence or authority : make more certain
list1.culpable: meriting condemnation or blame especially as wrong or harmful
list1.curtail: to make less by or as if by cutting off or away some part
list1.deference: respect and esteem due a superior or an elder; also : affected or ingratiating regard for another's wishes
list1.deleterious: harmful often in a subtle or unexpected way
list1.delineate: to describe, portray, or set forth with accuracy or in detail
list1.derision: the use of ridicule or scorn to show contempt
list1.desolate: devoid of inhabitants and visitors : deserted
list1.deterrent: serving to discourage, prevent, or inhibit : serving to deter
list1.digression: the act or an instance of leaving the main subject in an extended written or verbal expression of thought : the act or an instance of digressing in a discourse or other usually organized literary work
list1.diligent: characterized by steady, earnest, and energetic effort : painstaking
list1.disdain: a feeling of contempt for someone or something regarded as unworthy or inferior : scorn
list1.disparate: markedly distinct in quality or character
list1.dogmatic: characterized by or given to the expression of opinions very strongly or positively as if they were facts
list1.eclectic: composed of elements drawn from a variety of sources; also : heterogeneous
list1.eloquent: marked by forceful and fluent expression
list1.emulate: to strive to equal or excel
list1.enervate: lacking physical, mental, or moral vigor : enervated
list1.enigma: something hard to understand or explain
list1.equivocal: subject to two or more interpretations and usually used to mislead or confuse
list1.erudite: having or showing knowledge that is gained by studying : possessing or displaying erudition
list1.esoteric: designed for or understood by the specially initiated alone
list1.euphemism: the substitution of an agreeable or inoffensive word or expression for one that is harsh, indelicate, or otherwise unpleasant or taboo; also : the word or expression so substituted
list1.exacerbate: to make (something bad or unpleasant) worse
list1.exculpate: to clear from alleged fault or guilt
list1.exemplary: deserving imitation : commendable; also : deserving imitation because of excellence
list1.exonerate: to relieve of a responsibility, obligation, or hardship
list1.fastidious: extremely or excessively careful or detailed
list1.frugal: characterized by or reflecting thrift and efficiency in the use of resources
list1.furtive: done in a quiet and secretive way to avoid being noticed : surreptitious
list1.gregarious: enjoying the company of others : marked by or showing a liking for companionship : sociable
list1.gullible: easily duped or cheated; especially : quick to believe something that is not true
list1.haughty: blatantly and disdainfully proud : having or showing an attitude of superiority and contempt for people or things perceived to be inferior
list1.hypothetical: involving or being based on a suggested idea or theory : being or involving a hypothesis : conjectural
list1.immutable: not capable of or susceptible to change
list1.impetuous: marked by impulsive vehemence or passion
list1.indifferent: marked by a lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern for something : apathetic
list1.indigenous: produced, growing, living, or occurring natively or naturally in a particular region or environment
list1.inevitable: incapable of being avoided or evaded
list1.innocuous: not likely to bother or offend : inoffensive
list1.innovative: characterized by, tending to, or introducing innovations
list1.insipid: lacking in qualities that interest, stimulate, or challenge : dull, flat
list1.intrepid: characterized by resolute fearlessness, fortitude, and endurance
list1.ironic: relating to, containing, or constituting irony
list1.judicious: having, exercising, or characterized by sound judgment
list1.laconic: using or involving the use of a minimum of words : concise to the point of seeming rude or mysterious
list1.lethargic: of, relating to, or characterized by laziness or lack of energy : feeling or affected by lethargy : sluggish
list1.loquacious: given to fluent or excessive talk : garrulous
list1.magnanimous: showing or suggesting a generous and kind nature
